is a [Belgian association football](/wiki/Football_in_Belgium "Football in Belgium") club based in [Knokke](/wiki/Knokke "Knokke"), [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders"). The club currently play in the [Belgian National Division 1](/wiki/Belgian_National_Division_1 "Belgian National Division 1"), the third tier of Belgian football.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Belgian football club

Football club

Knokke

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | Royal Knokke Football Club |
| Founded | 1905; 121 years ago (1905) |
| Ground | Burgemeester Graaf Leopold Lippens Park,   [Knokke](/wiki/Knokke "Knokke") |
| Capacity | 3,000[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Chairman | Vincent Van Honsebrouck |
| Manager | Yves Van Borm |
| League | [National Division 1](/wiki/Belgian_National_Division_1 "Belgian National Division 1") |
| [2023–24](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_Belgian_National_Division_1 "2023–24 Belgian National Division 1") | National Division 1, 3rd of 20 |
| Website | <http://www.rkfc.be/> |

**Royal Knokke Football Club** is a [Belgian association football](/wiki/Football_in_Belgium "Football in Belgium") club based in [Knokke](/wiki/Knokke "Knokke"), [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders"). The club currently play in the [Belgian National Division 1](/wiki/Belgian_National_Division_1 "Belgian National Division 1"), the third tier of Belgian football.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Knokke_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The club was founded in 1905.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 2016–17, Knokke won the [Second Amateur Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Amateur_Division "Belgian Second Amateur Division") winning promotion to the [First Amateur Division](/wiki/Belgian_First_Amateur_Division "Belgian First Amateur Division"). After another successful season, Knokke finished second in the league standing, allowing them to participate in the final play-off round for promotion. Knokke won the final round, but did not apply for a licence for second-tier [Belgian First Division B](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_B "Belgian First Division B"), which meant that only [Lommel](/wiki/Lommel_S.K. "Lommel S.K.") promoted.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The following year, the club finished last in the table and relegated to the Second Amateur Division. There, they regained the league title and won promotion – albeit in a special way. Due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Belgium "COVID-19 pandemic in Belgium"), the [Royal Belgian Football Association](/wiki/Royal_Belgian_Football_Association "Royal Belgian Football Association") decided on 27 March 2020 to cancel all competitions and to determine the final rankings. Knokke had at that point played 24 games in the Second Amateur Division and finished on top with 63 points, forcing promotion back to the First Amateur Division.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
